title: fix: use Worker token for Worker-backed app deploys
author: ElliotEtag
pr_body_excerpt:
- Keeps the Apps token as the default for Pages app deploys.
- Passes the existing Myceli Worker token only to Worker-backed app deploys.
- Fixes the Websim deploy failure after adding deployTarget: worker.

Checks:
- bash -n apps/_scripts/deploy.sh
- git diff --check

gist.headline: Worker bees get the right keys
gist.blurb: Fixed a deployment hiccup where Worker-backed apps were getting the wrong access tokens. Websim and other worker apps are back to deploying smoothly!
gist.summary: Updated the automatic app deployment workflow to pass the Myceli Worker token specifically for Worker-backed apps while keeping the Apps token default for Pages.
gist.impact: Worker-backed apps like Websim will now deploy successfully without token authentication failures.
gist.visual_concept: Polly the bee acting as a dispatcher, handing the correct glowing key (token) to a worker robot, while another robot (Pages) holds a different key.
gist.image_prompt: Cozy pixel art scene of Polly the bee mascot wearing a tiny security guard hat, handing a glowing lime green (#ecf874) digital key to a chunky worker robot. Another robot nearby holds a different pastel-colored key. Soft pastel gradient background. Warm lighting, lo-fi 8-bit aesthetic like Stardew Valley, chunky sprites, emotionally warm.
